_doc: Labelled sample PR diffs encoding the core-store schema migration
convention (migrations/README.md). Each fixture is a real unified diff
against this repo's current src DDL — they apply cleanly with
`git apply --check` from the repo root — and `expect` is the verdict the
schema-migration audit gate (landing separately) must produce for a PR
pass — the gate exits 0 (additive change, artifact shipped, or the
touched DDL is out of scope: Better Auth / extension-owned)
fail — the gate exits non-zero (destructive in-scope change with no
in the same diff — the legacy migrations/NNNN_*.sql psql form
is retired for new migrations and also fails)
The gate's classifier test must run every fixture and assert its label.
When the convention gains a new destructive/additive case, add a fixture
here in the same PR that documents it.

fixtures.description: Drops usage_events.agent_label (CREATE-DDL text, legacy DO-block adds, and an inline DROP COLUMN) without shipping a migrations/ artifact.

Narrows usage_events.cost_usd numeric(12,8) -> numeric(12,4) and ships the node-pg-migrate runner module migrations/core/core__0012_narrow-usage-events-cost-usd.mjs plus its manifest entry in the same diff (the artifact form adopted in cinatra#116).

Same cost_usd retype but shipping the RETIRED legacy psql artifact (migrations/0012_*.sql + manifest entry). The core runner never executes loose .sql files, so the gate rejects the form and demands a migrations/core/core__NNNN_*.mjs module.

Adds a VALID migrations/core/core__0005_*.mjs module carrying destructive SQL with no manifest entry and no in-scope schema-file diff. The runner executes every valid core/ module regardless of the manifest, so migration-state inconsistency must fail standalone — not only when a destructive drizzle-store diff is present.

Edits migrations/manifest.json _doc wording without touching the migrations array. Legitimate without a module — the standalone inconsistency rule fires only when the array GAINS entries with no matching module (rewrites/removals of existing entries are integrity failures via the append-only prefix check).
Adds a nullable agent_templates.icon column (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS) and a partial index — no data impact, no artifact required.
Removes a Better Auth additional user field (destructive-shaped, but auth schema is owned by getMigrations and guarded by auth-schema-drift); the gate must ignore it.

fixtures.category: out of scope (extension DDL), bundled with in-scope additive
fixtures.description: Adds destructive-shaped extension-owned SQL (a DROP COLUMN example in the extension migration host, the #118 out-of-scope surface) bundled with an in-scope additive index; the out-of-scope hunks must not trip the gate. Refreshed in #118 when the JSON-DSL files it originally touched were retired.
